confirmed.summary: An existing test hardcodes the phantom allowlist entry and will break red when FR-003 edits it — not in plan scope
confirmed.evidence: tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:275 lists "src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py" in its own critical_path_modules copy and asserts `assert module in enforced_run` (lines 286-287). The plan's touched-files (plan.md:34-40) names only test_ci_quality_path_filters.py and test_workflow_coherence.py.
confirmed.recommendation: Add tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py to the FR-003/IC-02 scope. When the ci-quality.yml entry is edited/removed, this hand-rolled second authority must be updated in lockstep (or the fix cannot go green). Follow the in-file precedent (lines 279-282, the `next/*` vacuous-entry removal by mission ci-suite-map-bind FR-004e): replace the phantom entry with the corrected path or drop it with an inline rationale comment. Note the irony that FR-004 forbids 'a hand-rolled second bash-array-in-YAML parser' while this exact regex-based authority already exists and is left unconsolidated.

verdict.reasoning:
The finding withstands adversarial scrutiny; every factual claim verifies against the live tree, and the one unstated precondition that would have refuted it actually holds in its favor.

VERIFIED FACTS: (1) tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:275 hardcodes "src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py" in its own critical_path_modules copy (270-285), and 286-287 do an unguarded `for module in critical_path_modules: assert module in enforced_run`. (2) enforced_run is regex-parsed from the live ci-quality.yml enforced diff-coverage step, whose line 2709 still carries 'src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py' — so the assertion passes today. (3) The plan's touched-files (plan.md:36-39) lists only prompt_metadata.py, test_prompt_retention.py, ci-quality.yml, and test_ci_quality_path_filters.py; a grep across the entire dossier (plan/spec/tasks/checklists) shows test_diff_coverage_policy.py is referenced nowhere.

CRUX THE FINDING DIDN'T PROVE BUT WHICH HOLDS: the phantom assertion sits behind `if decision != "tighten_workflow": pytest.skip()` (230-231). The WP03 validation report (kitty-specs/068-.../wp03-validation-report.md:28) is `[x] tighten_workflow`, so the block is LIVE, not skipped. Had the decision been close_with_evidence, the whole block would skip and the finding would collapse — but it does not.

DETERMINISM: FR-003 (spec.md:14/26/36, SC-002:58) mandates removing/correcting the bogus entry (a file that never existed in git history); FR-004's new existence guard would red if it stayed. I simulated both FR-003 branches against the live workflow: remove → phantom not in enforced_run (RED); replace-with-real-path → also RED. Either branch strips the literal string, flipping line 287. I also re-evaluated every other assertion in the test (advisory || true, --fail-under=90, coverage-*.xml, fast-tests-charter/core-misc needs-edges, quality-gate dependency, non-critical exclusions, all other critical modules) — all currently pass, so the test is genuinely green now and truly transitions to red (not already-red for another reason). Precedent confirmed: lines 279-282 record that ci-suite-map-bind FR-004e edited this same hardcoded list in lockstep when removing src/specify_cli/next/*, and .gates/post-spec.json:45 independently cites this file as a known second authority. FR-004's "no hand-rolled second bash-array-in-YAML parser" mandate is genuinely ironic against this pre-existing regex+hardcoded copy.

SEVERITY CALIBRATION to medium (from high): the consequence is deterministic and real (a tracked, un-skipped test WILL red, blocking the mission from going green), but the blast radius is a single one-line lockstep edit mirroring an established in-repo precedent, and it is gate-caught — any full-suite run or CI reds before merge, so it cannot silently escape to production. Real defect, bounded blast radius, caught by gates = medium, not high. The recommendation (add tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py to FR-003/IC-02 scope and update the hardcoded copy per the lines 279-282 precedent) is correct and actionable.

confirmed.summary: FR-003's leading determination candidate is provably wrong; the real home was found during gating but never written back to the spec
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:14 leads with 'candidate: src/specify_cli/acceptance/__init__.py::detect_mission_slug', but that function's docstring (src/specify_cli/acceptance/__init__.py:458) states 'Require an explicit mission slug; no auto-detection' — it detects nothing. The post-spec gate's own refutation (.gates/post-spec.json) already established the real successor: 'lanes/branch_naming.py::parse_mission_slug_from_branch, core/vcs/detection.py'. The doctrine doc (git-operations-matrix.md:28) describes the lost logic as '_detect_from_branch()' branch detection, absent from src.
confirmed.recommendation: Correct spec.md:14 grounding: drop the disproven detect_mission_slug candidate and point the FR-003 determination at the verified home (core/vcs/detection.py::parse_mission_slug_from_branch and lanes/branch_naming.py). FR-003 forbids 'bare removal without recorded proof'; seeding the implementer with a candidate that does no detection risks a bogus recorded determination that satisfies the letter of FR-003 while re-committing the exact silent-coverage-drop #2443 targets.

verdict.reasoning:
The finding withstands adversarial scrutiny against the live repo/artifacts. All four load-bearing evidentiary claims verify exactly:

1. spec.md:14 leads FR-003's grounding with "candidate: src/specify_cli/acceptance/__init__.py::detect_mission_slug" (the other two, core/stale_detection.py and core/project_resolver.py, follow as "also"). None of the three is the gate-verified home.
2. detect_mission_slug (def at acceptance/__init__.py:458) has the docstring "Require an explicit mission slug; no auto-detection" and its body discards all context args (`_ = (repo_root, env, cwd, announce_fallback)`) and calls _require_explicit_feature — it provably performs no detection.
3. The mission's own post-spec gate (.gates/post-spec.json, /dropped[1]/why_refuted) states verbatim: "The moved logic is real (lanes/branch_naming.py::parse_mission_slug_from_branch, core/vcs/detection.py)." I independently confirmed those do genuine branch-based mission-slug detection (branch_naming.py:778; vcs/detection.py:158-161 calls it to resolve the mission from the branch/worktree).
4. git-operations-matrix.md:28 pins the lost logic to branch detection via `git branch --show-current` → core/mission_detection.py::_detect_from_branch(); _detect_from_branch is absent from src/ and core/mission_detection.py has 0 commits in all history.

Not stale: the gate ran 19:16, spec.md was edited 19:17 (commit 5d310e891 "Fold post-spec findings ... FR-003 recorded-determination + no-bare-removal"), so the author had the gate's verified home in hand while editing and still left the disproven lead uncorrected and the real home omitted entirely.

Not already-handled: the FR-003 strengthening (recorded-determination / no-bare-removal, responding to confirmed[0]) addresses the fakeability requirement but not the misleading grounding. FR-004's existence guard passes regardless of which home is chosen, and FR-003's determination is an explicitly labeled "human call," so anchoring on the non-detecting seeded lead is a realistic path to a plausible-but-wrong recorded determination that satisfies the letter of FR-003 while re-committing the exact silent-coverage-drop #2443 targets. The finding is additive to confirmed[0], not a duplicate.

Only cosmetic imprecisions: the docstring is at ~line 466 not 458 (458 is the def), and the recommendation writes "core/vcs/detection.py::parse_mission_slug_from_branch" when that function lives in lanes/branch_naming.py — but it also names lanes/branch_naming.py separately, mirroring the gate's own phrasing, so both correct files are cited. Neither undermines the finding.

Severity: medium is appropriate. It is not high — the spec has independent guardrails (FR-003's no-bare-removal language, Assumptions:67 telling the implementer to locate the home) and FR-004's guard still functions, so an alert implementer could still find the real home. It is not low — a known-wrong LEAD candidate that does no detection, when the correct answer already sits in the mission's own gate file, combined with the "human call" nature of the FR-003 determination, is a realistic route to re-committing the exact #2443 harm. It sits at the medium/low boundary but the direct mapping to the mission's core risk keeps it at medium.

confirmed.summary: Plan places the FR-004 guard in a file that has no access to the canonical parser, based on a stale grounding claim that it iterates the allowlist
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:14 claims 'test_workflow_coherence.py:231 + test_ci_quality_path_filters.py:205 already iterate it [the allowlist]', and plan.md:39 puts the new guard in 'tests/architectural/test_ci_quality_path_filters.py  # IC-02: NEW existence guard (reuses _diff_cover_critical_paths)'. LIVE: test_ci_quality_path_filters.py:205 is test_diff_coverage_critical_paths_include_canonical_runtime() which uses raw substring asserts ('assert "src/runtime/next/*" in run_script') and never imports _gate_coverage. `grep -rln _diff_cover_critical_paths tests/` returns ONLY _gate_coverage.py and test_workflow_coherence.py — test_ci_quality_path_filters.py does not consume the canonical parser at all. test_workflow_coherence.py already imports `from tests.architectural import _gate_coverage as gc` and iterates `model.diff_cover_critical_paths` (:239).
confirmed.recommendation: Land the existence guard in tests/architectural/test_workflow_coherence.py next to test_diff_cover_critical_paths_are_cov_backed_live (:231), iterating the already-parsed `model.diff_cover_critical_paths`. Correct the stale grounding in spec.md:14 and :54 (and plan.md:39) that says test_ci_quality_path_filters.py:205 iterates the allowlist — it does not, and adding the guard there tempts the hand-rolled second parser FR-004 explicitly forbids.

verdict.reasoning: Every mechanical claim verifies against live code. spec.md:14 states test_workflow_coherence.py:231 + test_ci_quality_path_filters.py:205 "already iterate it" (the allowlist), under an "Existing infra to reuse" banner. That claim is factually FALSE for test_ci_quality_path_filters.py:205: it is test_diff_coverage_critical_paths_include_canonical_runtime(), which does raw substring asserts against a job run-script string (assert "'src/runtime/next/*'" in run_script). A grep confirms that file references NONE of diff_cover_critical_paths / _ci_quality_model / _gate_coverage / gc. (output: "(none)"); it uses its own yaml.safe_load + _job_run_script idiom — the exact hand-rolled parser FR-004 forbids. grep -rln _diff_cover_critical_paths tests/ returns ONLY _gate_coverage.py (parser def at :442) and test_workflow_coherence.py, which imports _gate_coverage as gc (:36) and iterates model.diff_cover_critical_paths (:239) inside :231. plan.md:39 places the new FR-004 existence guard in test_ci_quality_path_filters.py — the file with zero parser linkage.\n\nAdversarial counterpoints weighed: (1) The headline "no access to the canonical parser" is slightly overstated since both files sit in tests/architectural/ and the parser is importable; but the finding's body correctly narrows this to "does not consume the canonical parser at all," which is the load-bearing, accurate claim. (2) plan.md:32 does carry the FR-004 guardrail ("reusing that canonical parser (do not re-parse the bash array)"), which mitigates the worst outcome and keeps this from being high. Neither refutes the finding: the spec grounding is objectively wrong for the file the plan selected, and that false "already iterates it" premise steers the implementer toward the file's native raw-substring/yaml idiom — the anti-pattern FR-004/SC-002 forbid — when a cleaner, already-wired home (test_workflow_coherence.py:231) exists. Severity medium is appropriate: a factually false spec grounding drove a concrete, suboptimal file-placement decision; the fix (land the guard beside test_diff_cover_critical_paths_are_cov_backed_live and correct spec.md:14) is sound and specific. Not stale/misread/already-handled.

confirmed.summary: FR-003 fix will turn an existing pinned test red; that file is missing from the plan's touched-files set (under-sizing)
confirmed.evidence: tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:270-287 hard-asserts the bogus entry is present: line 275 `"src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py",` inside `critical_path_modules`, then line 286-287 `for module in critical_path_modules: assert module in enforced_run`. This test carries pytestmark integration/git_repo so it runs in CI. The plan's 'Project Structure (files touched)' (plan.md:35-40) lists only prompt_metadata.py, test_prompt_retention.py, ci-quality.yml, test_ci_quality_path_filters.py — NOT test_diff_coverage_policy.py. When FR-003 removes/corrects the ci-quality.yml:2709 entry, this assert goes red. Precedent is in the same file: lines 279-282 already document an identical prior removal (`src/specify_cli/next/*`), proving this literal must be edited whenever an allowlist entry changes.
confirmed.recommendation: Add tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py to the plan's touched-files set and to IC-02's scope; the FR-003 change must update its `critical_path_modules` literal in the same diff. Better: migrate that hand-rolled Python list to consume the canonical `_diff_cover_critical_paths` parser (per the mission's own FR-004 / canonical-source charter), so this second copy of the allowlist can't silently rot again.

verdict.reasoning:
VERDICT: real=true. The finding withstands adversarial refutation; every load-bearing claim was verified against the live repo, and the failure mechanism was reproduced deterministically.

WHAT I CONFIRMED (live):
- tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:270-287 hardcodes a second copy of the allowlist. Line 275 lists "src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py" inside critical_path_modules; lines 286-287 loop `for module in critical_path_modules: assert module in enforced_run`, where enforced_run is parsed from the LIVE ci-quality.yml (line 233 `_WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text()`, regex at 256-262 capturing the "diff-coverage (critical-path, enforced)" step). Confirmed the bogus entry is at ci-quality.yml:2709 inside exactly that step (step spans 2692-2741; array 2704-2715; `--include "${critical_paths[@]}"` at 2741).
- Precedent claim is accurate: lines 279-282 document an identical prior removal (`src/specify_cli/next/*`, ci-suite-map-bind FR-004e), proving this literal list must be edited whenever an allowlist entry changes.
- The plan's touched-files set (plan.md:35-40) lists only prompt_metadata.py, test_prompt_retention.py, ci-quality.yml, and tests/architectural/test_ci_quality_path_filters.py. IC-02's scope (plan.md:30-32) names only test_ci_quality_path_filters.py. tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py is genuinely absent from both. tasks/ is still empty (.gitkeep + README only); grep found zero references to this test in the mission dossier.

I ATTACKED THE STRONGEST REFUTATION AND IT FAILED: the assert sits inside test_tighten_workflow_passes_large_pr_sample, which skips (line 230-231) unless the WP03 validation report's recorded decision is `tighten_workflow`. I checked the LIVE report: `[ ] close_with_evidence` / `[x] tighten_workflow` (wp03-validation-report.md:27-28, last set by mission 065 commit ea12637d7). So the test does NOT skip. (Historical mission-068 review notes saying "1 correctly skipped" reflect the earlier close_with_evidence draft, since superseded — a stale artifact, not the live state.) I reproduced the exact test logic in a standalone script: decision=tighten_workflow -> assert loop RUNS; current ci-quality.yml -> all 10 modules present -> test GREEN; after removing the mission_detection.py line per FR-003 -> that module is the sole missing one -> test RED.

The removal is not optional: FR-004 adds an existence guard requiring every allowlist entry to resolve to a real file, and mission_detection.py does not exist on disk (spec.md:14, confirmed). SC-002 demands that guard be "green after," which forces the bogus literal out of the allowlist. So both FR-003 and FR-004 guarantee the string leaves ci-quality.yml, guaranteeing this pinned test goes red. The plan under-sizes by omitting the file that must change in the same diff.

SEVERITY: adjusted high -> medium. Real and correctly diagnosed, but the consequence is a loud, self-revealing red test caught at implement/CI gate time (not a silent or shipped defect), the blast radius is one file / one literal edit, and a known-good remediation pattern already exists in the same file (the next/* precedent). It stays above low because the plan's touched-files set is the authoritative sizing/lane-scoping artifact and an implementer following it literally (running only the two test dirs the plan names: tests/architectural/ and tests/specify_cli/review/) would miss tests/release/ and only discover the break in full CI — avoidable rework. The recommendation is sound and thematically aligned with the mission's own canonical-source charter (FR-004): this is a second hand-rolled copy of the allowlist that will rot exactly as the mission is trying to prevent, so migrating it to consume _diff_cover_critical_paths is the durable fix.

Key paths: /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2439/tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:26,206,230-231,270-287; /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2439/.github/workflows/ci-quality.yml:2692-2741 (entry at 2709); /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2439/kitty-specs/068-post-merge-reliability-and-release-hardening/wp03-validation-report.md:27-28; /home/jeroennouws/dev/sk-missions/2439/kitty-specs/landing-hygiene-01KWYRE1/plan.md:30-40.

confirmed.summary: Two doctrine/doc references encode the phantom mission_detection.py (one even names a non-existent function); FR-003's blast radius is under-scoped
confirmed.evidence: grep for `core/mission_detection.py` outside kitty-specs returns four hits: ci-quality.yml:2709 (in scope), tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:275 (finding 1), plus two UNSCOPED docs — docs/plans/testing/ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md:46 `- src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py — mission detection`, and src/doctrine/skills/spec-kitty-git-workflow/references/git-operations-matrix.md:28 `| git branch --show-current | Branch detection | core/mission_detection.py | _detect_from_branch() |`. `_detect_from_branch()` does not exist anywhere in src (grep returned nothing). FR-003 explicitly requires 'determine where the mission-detection logic actually lives' (spec.md:36), yet the plan scopes the fix to ci-quality.yml only (plan.md:38), leaving these phantom references — exactly the silent rot #2443 targets.
confirmed.recommendation: Fold doc reconciliation into FR-003's recorded determination: correct or delete the two references (docs/plans/testing/ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md:46 and the doctrine skill git-operations-matrix.md:28) as part of the same change. Note that touching src/doctrine/ requires running the terminology guard (tests/architectural/test_no_legacy_terminology.py) per repo policy — account for that in sizing.

verdict.reasoning: The finding withstands adversarial scrutiny against the live repo. Every claim verified: (1) All four grep hits are live and exact. (2) mission_detection.py is genuinely phantom — `find` returns nothing and the only `mission_detection` string in src/ is the one doctrine matrix line; spec.md:14 itself confirms it never existed in git history. (3) `_detect_from_branch()` exists nowhere in code; furthermore the matrix row (git-operations-matrix.md:28) is doubly wrong — the real home of `git branch --show-current` branch detection is core/git_ops.py::get_current_branch() (git_ops.py:136-150), not core/mission_detection.py::_detect_from_branch(). (4) Not already-handled: neither stale doc nor the phantom function is referenced anywhere in the mission dossier; plan.md's files-touched list (35-40) is ci-quality.yml + one test only, and plan/spec never touch docs/plans or src/doctrine. (5) The coupling is tight, not scope-creep: ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md is the tuning-notes companion doc for the SAME diff-cover --include allowlist FR-003 corrects — it calls ci-quality.yml the 'authoritative list' (line 42) and then mirrors the phantom entry (line 46). It silently rots exactly as US2/#2443 targets. (6) Critically, FR-003 (spec.md:36) and SC-002 (spec.md:58) already OBLIGATE the mission to record 'the concrete determination of where the mission-detection logic lives' — the very determination that corrects the two docs; applying it in only one of three phantom-encoding sites is textbook under-scoped blast radius. (7) The sizing note is accurate — CLAUDE.md mandates the terminology guard for src/doctrine/ edits and tests/architectural/test_no_legacy_terminology.py exists. The strongest refutation ('narrow mission, docs out of scope') fails because the recommendation folds into FR-003's already-mandated determination rather than spawning new work. Severity held at medium: it is doc drift with no runtime/gate-enforcement impact (caps below high), but one reference ships via src/doctrine/ to every consumer project through `spec-kitty upgrade` teaching a phantom file+function for a real git op, and the other is the direct companion doc to the fix — with near-zero marginal fix cost given the mandated determination (keeps it above low).

confirmed.summary: Spec's FR-003 candidate landing spot (detect_mission_slug) is itself stale — it no longer detects and has zero internal callers
confirmed.evidence: spec.md:14 and spec.md:67 point the implementer at `src/specify_cli/acceptance/__init__.py::detect_mission_slug` as the likely current home of the mission-detection logic. But that function (acceptance/__init__.py:458-485) now REQUIRES an explicit slug — docstring line 466 'Require an explicit mission slug; no auto-detection' — and grep shows zero callers inside src (only its own def and an __all__ export at :1729). The historical detection that motivated the entry (commit 2bb543ac9 'Use feature-level mission detection in mission current command', 2026-01-25) landed in src/specify_cli/cli/commands/mission.py, which now contains no detect/branch logic either (grep empty). So the spec's parenthetical hint sends the implementer toward a dead end.
confirmed.recommendation: Update the spec/plan hint to reflect that the auto-detection logic appears to have been removed (candidate outcome: FR-003's 'remove the entry with recorded proof of existing coverage'), rather than steering toward detect_mission_slug. This keeps the determination fast and avoids re-pinning another stale path.

verdict.reasoning:
Every factual claim verifies against live code. detect_mission_slug (src/specify_cli/acceptance/__init__.py:458-485) has docstring line 466 "Require an explicit mission slug; no auto-detection", just delegates to _require_explicit_feature, and does no detection. grep confirms zero internal src callers (only def :458 and __all__ export :1729; no dynamic .detect_mission_slug access). It is independently corroborated as dead: tests/architectural/test_no_dead_symbols.py:132 grandfathers specify_cli.acceptance::detect_mission_slug into the dead-symbol baseline. Commit 2bb543ac9 (2026-01-25, "Use feature-level mission detection in mission current command") is real and added _detect_current_feature() to mission.py; mission.py is now a 10-line shim re-exporting mission_type.py, and the moved _detect_current_feature (mission_type.py:172-181) now unconditionally returns None ("no auto-detection") — so the detection class was gutted system-wide, strengthening the finding. core/mission_detection.py never existed in git history (confirmed). The recommendation is sound and useful: it pre-computes FR-003's mandated determination and lands on "remove the entry with recorded proof of existing coverage."

Downgraded medium->low because the finding slightly overstates the spec's steering. spec.md:14 labels detect_mission_slug a "candidate" (one of three; stale_detection.py and project_resolver.py also listed), and spec.md:67 does not name it at all. FR-003 (spec.md:36), the #2443 Edge Cases (spec.md:26), and SC-002 already require the implementer to determine where the logic actually lives and explicitly pre-authorize "remove the entry" with recorded proof. The spec is therefore self-correcting by design: a spec-compliant implementer reaches the same conclusion. The stale hint causes at most minor wasted investigation, not a wrong outcome — this is a spec-accuracy nit / helpful early determination, not a boundary defect that misdirects the implementer. Real but low severity.

Key paths: kitty-specs/landing-hygiene-01KWYRE1/spec.md (lines 14, 26, 36, 58, 67); src/specify_cli/acceptance/__init__.py:458-485, :1729; src/specify_cli/cli/commands/mission.py; src/specify_cli/cli/commands/mission_type.py:172-181; tests/architectural/test_no_dead_symbols.py:132; .github/workflows/ci-quality.yml:2709.

dropped.summary: Phantom module reference lives in >=3 more live authorities (incl. a shipped doctrine artifact) that FR-004's guard cannot see
dropped.why_refuted:
Evidence is factually accurate (verified live), but the finding does not withstand refutation as a medium finding against this mission.

VERIFIED FACTS: The phantom module `specify_cli.core.mission_detection` (nonexistent; never in git history) does appear at pyproject.toml:307 (dead entry in the [[tool.mypy.overrides]] strict-quarantine list, block opens L286, `module = [` L288), at src/doctrine/skills/spec-kitty-git-workflow/references/git-operations-matrix.md:28 (a shipped doctrine artifact; the referenced function `_detect_from_branch` is ALSO phantom — real logic is in core/git_ops.py:139), and at docs/plans/testing/ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md:46. FR-004's guard reuses `_diff_cover_critical_paths`, which parses only ci-quality.yml's allowlist, so it is genuinely blind to all three, and the plan does not touch them (so not 'already-handled'). The '>=3 more authorities' count is literally true.

WHY IT FAILS ADVERSARIAL SCRUTINY:
1) Goal-scope misframing (the load-bearing claim). The finding says these undercut the mission goal 'a moved/renamed module can't silently drop enforcement again.' That goal (spec.md:20-21, US2) is coverage-ENFORCEMENT-specific — the diff-coverage --include allowlist. The mypy quarantine (pyproject.toml:307) and the git-ops doctrine doc have zero relationship to coverage enforcement and cannot 'silently drop coverage enforcement.' The third (ci-coverage-gate-tuning.md) explicitly names ci-quality.yml as 'the authoritative list' (L42-43) and is a historical 'as of WP07' tuning note. So the goal is not undercut.
2) Severity overstated. The leftovers are cosmetic: a dead mypy override that mypy does not even flag (warn_unused_configs is NOT set — confirmed) and documentation drift. Zero functional/correctness/security impact → low, not medium.
3) 'Same root, same fix moment' is a stretch — the authorities are joined only by a coincidental phantom NAME. The git-ops doctrine-doc drift is a genuine but ORTHOGONAL bug (phantom file + phantom function, real logic long moved to core/git_ops.py) unrelated to a coverage-allowlist mission; it should be filed separately, not folded as evidence this mission is incomplete. The mission is explicitly scoped as 'two small, independent hygiene fixes.'
4) The finding's own phantom-map is incomplete: it MISSES tests/release/test_diff_coverage_policy.py:275, which hard-asserts `assert 'src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py' in enforced_run` and will actively BREAK when FR-003 corrects/removes the ci-quality.yml entry. That is the only reference with real coupling to the delivered fix, and the finding did not flag it — undermining its claim to 'close the whole class.'

NET: accurate grep facts wrapped in an overstated mission-goal-defect argument that is actually optional campsite cleaning plus an orthogonal doc bug. Central significance claim is a scope-misframing, severity is inflated (low, not medium), and it omits the one mission-coupled surface. Default-to-false stands; adjusted_severity=low reflects the genuine but cosmetic factual kernel.

dropped.summary: Retention prune is scoped to the current WP dir only; per-WP/mission directory count still grows unbounded over a repo's lifetime
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's mechanical facts are accurate (plan.md:27 prunes only "that WP dir"; prompt_metadata.py:116-123 builds <repo-id>/<mission>/<wp>/<invocation>.md so each mission/WP is a fresh sibling dir; sole writer is workflow.py:2774 — verified as the only caller of write_review_prompt_with_metadata). But the material thesis does not withstand scrutiny and defaults to real=false for five reasons.

1) ALREADY ADJUDICATED, NO NEW EVIDENCE. This identical finding was raised at the post-spec gate and DROPPED as real=false (.gates/post-spec.json:60-61: "US1 promises repo-level boundedness but the cap is applied per (repo,mission,WP) directory, so total repo growth stays unbounded and stale WP/mission dirs are never cleaned"). The post-plan re-raise adds only a plan.md:27 citation, which is not new evidence of a defect — plan.md:27 merely implements the per-WP pruning that SC-001 (spec.md:57) and the US1 independent test (spec.md:18) already specify. Re-litigating a falsified hypothesis without new evidence is what DIR-003 forbids.

2) STORAGE MISLOCATED. The finding frames growth as "over a repo's lifetime" under the repo, but the tree is rooted at tempfile.gettempdir() = /tmp (prompt_metadata.py:117; TMPDIR unset on this host, confirmed). It is OS-managed temp keyed by <repo-id>, not the working tree, with a reboot/tmp-cleaner backstop. "Unbounded over a repo's lifetime" overstates a temp-namespace residual.

3) NO PLAN-VS-SPEC DRIFT. FR-002's "scoped to the spec-kitty-review-prompts/<repo-id>/… tree only" is a blast-radius/containment safety boundary (paired with NFR-002 fail-safety), not a directive to sweep the whole <repo-id> tree. The binding acceptance criteria (SC-001 spec.md:57; US1 independent test spec.md:18) both define retention per (repo, mission, WP). plan.md:27's "that WP dir" is consistent with them — the plan narrows nothing the spec did not already scope.

4) STRONGER REMEDIATION EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE. Pruning stale sibling WP/mission dirs is a cross-directory GC over subtrees the write never touches; spec.md:63 Out of Scope explicitly excludes "a global GC daemon" and "any broader review-prompt redesign." The finding recommends work the mission deliberately fenced off.

5) MILDER REMEDIATION LARGELY SATISFIED; MAGNITUDE NEGLIGIBLE. The "note it explicitly" ask is substantially met — spec.md:63 draws the boundary and the post-spec gate recorded the adjudication of this exact residual. The residual is capped-set × count-of-distinct-mission×WP-leaf-dirs, growing only with distinct missions/WPs actually reviewed — precisely NOT the per-WP invocation-count explosion the mission targets and eliminates.

Kernel of truth: US1's aspirational title ("don't grow unbounded") is looser than the delivered per-WP mechanism since one dimension (leaf-dir count in /tmp) stays uncapped — a low-severity editorial wording nit, not a standing defect. Coupled to a mislocated-storage claim, an out-of-scope GC recommendation, and being a no-new-evidence re-raise of an already-refuted finding, it does not clearly withstand scrutiny. real=false; residual value is at most low/editorial.

dropped.summary: The live allowlist is glob-heavy; the 'every entry resolves to an existing file' guard as specified would red 8 of 10 entries and break the gate
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's factual scaffolding is accurate (parser returns 8 globs + 2 concrete files verbatim; src/specify_cli/core/mission_detection.py is genuinely dangling; the 8 glob dirs all exist; line citations correct), but its dispositive claim — the guard "as specified would red 8 of 10 entries and break the gate" and "can never go green even after the dangling entry is fixed" — does not withstand scrutiny.

1) Strawman: the spec never mandates Path(entry).exists(). FR-004 says the guard asserts each entry "resolves to a real file"; SC-002 says "references only existing files." "Resolves" is an abstract verb that for a glob naturally means "matches >=1 real file." The finding imputes a naive glob-blind implementation and refutes that, not the spec.

2) Refuted by live sibling code the spec directs reuse of. FR-004 requires landing the guard alongside test_workflow_coherence.py/test_ci_quality_path_filters.py and reusing the canonical parser. test_workflow_coherence.py already contains the exact glob-aware resolution the finding "recommends adding": critical_path_backed_by (L89) strips '/*', and glob_is_live (L78-86) does fnmatch-based "glob matches >=1 tracked path." The convention is dominant in the precise file the guard lands in.

3) The recommendation is already in the spec: FR-004 "Red-first against the current dangling entry" and SC-002 "red against the pre-fix dangling entry and green after" already scope red-first to the concrete dangling entry, not the whole array.

4) "Break the gate" conflates the new pytest existence-guard with the actual CI diff-coverage gate. The real gate uses the globs natively via shell git diff and diff-cover --include and is untouched by pytest resolution semantics. Under the mandated red-first workflow, the guard "can never go green" is self-refuting — making it green after correcting the allowlist is the implementer's deliverable, so glob false-reds are caught and corrected in the same cycle; nothing broken ships.

Residual value is only a harmless low/informational clarification (spell out glob-vs-concrete resolution), already modeled by glob_is_live — not a medium boundary defect. Default real=false holds.

dropped.summary: Plan drops one of the two guard homes the spec names and mislabels an existing 20 KB file as NEW
dropped.why_refuted:
The finding's raw disk facts are all accurate (test_ci_quality_path_filters.py exists at exactly 20538 bytes and is git-tracked; test_workflow_coherence.py's test_diff_cover_critical_paths_are_cov_backed_live iterates model.diff_cover_critical_paths; no existing test asserts disk existence, so the FR-004 guard is genuinely new). But both load-bearing CONCLUSIONS are misreads and do not withstand scrutiny.

Claim A ("plan drops one of the two guard homes the spec names") over-reads the spec. FR-004 (spec.md:37) requires ONE guard "reusing the canonical parser ... added alongside the existing critical_paths tests (test_workflow_coherence.py/test_ci_quality_path_filters.py)". spec.md:14 frames both files as tests that "already iterate" the allowlist — sibling reference points, not two mandated placements. "Alongside A/B" means co-located with A and B (both live in tests/architectural/), not "duplicated into both." A single guard in test_ci_quality_path_filters.py is alongside test_workflow_coherence.py and, because it consumes the single canonical allowlist via _diff_cover_critical_paths, fully closes FR-004. Duplicating into both would be redundant, not required. The plan implements the spec; it does not drop a home.

Claim B ("mislabels an existing 20 KB file as NEW") misreads the plan. Line 39's "# IC-02: NEW existence guard (reuses _diff_cover_critical_paths)" has "NEW" modifying "existence guard" (the test function), not the file; the section header is explicitly "Project Structure (files touched)"; and IC-02 body (plan.md:32) says "add an existence assertion ... Land it alongside" — add/land, not create. The guard genuinely is new (confirmed: no disk-existence check exists today), so the label is accurate. The parallel line 37 (test_prompt_retention.py "NEW retention tests") is a file that truly doesn't exist, confirming "NEW" consistently tags the added tests/guard, not the file.

The "may mislead the implementer" concern is speculative and already pre-empted by IC-02's "add an existence assertion ... Land it alongside" plus "(reuses _diff_cover_critical_paths)". No coverage gap results, and the recommendation is cosmetic. Severity noted as low only as a ceiling; the finding does not survive refutation, so real=false.
